02 / 24
Executive Summary

METRIX turns workforce data entry into governed operational control.

The product centralizes employee contracts, campaign records, working hours, training activity and leave visibility into one controlled interface, with validation rules and permission enforcement at both frontend and backend level.

01

Operational source of truth

Contracts, campaigns, hours and leaves are managed or surfaced through a single application layer instead of fragmented manual files.

02

Controlled change

Users see only what their role, page access, campaign access and workforce visibility allow.

03

Payroll-quality controls

Critical errors are blocked before they become downstream payroll corrections.

Executive summaryMETRIX
03 / 24
Business Context

Why the platform matters

Before METRIX

  • Manual corrections across contracts, WH and leave data
  • Higher risk of duplicate or overlapping records
  • Unclear ownership of operational changes
  • Late discovery of payroll-impacting exceptions

METRIX control layer

  • Authenticated access to protected modules
  • Business rules applied before database writes
  • Permission-scoped views and exports
  • Exception reports for post-factum conflicts

Target outcome

  • Cleaner workforce dataset
  • Fewer manual checks before payroll
  • Faster operational review
  • Clearer accountability and auditability
Business rationaleFrom data entry to control
04 / 24
Platform Scope

Core product modules

Employee Contracts

Full CRUD, contract status, KPI dashboard, Dedicated / Shared rules and overlap protection.

Campaign List

Managed registry of campaign records, active/deactivated logic and project source for dynamic WH pages.

Working Hours

Project-based working hour entry with split shifts, breaks, overnight shifts, closures and agent/campaign selection.

Training Tracker

Onboarding and training lifecycle tracking with filters, summary KPIs, pass status and production milestones.

Training Hours

Daily Training/Nesting hour capture for trainees before production, governed by payment month and eligibility controls.

Leaves Taken

Read-only leave explorer from Odoo, expanded by day and filterable by date, campaign, position and agent.

WH Leave Conflicts

Exception report that identifies saved working hours overlapping leave records added later in HR systems.

Settings & Governance

User permissions, role definitions, workplan closures, page access and campaign access administration.

Product modulesCurrent platform coverage
05 / 24
Enterprise Architecture

Controlled request lifecycle

The application is designed so authentication, authorization and business validation happen before data is written or exposed to users.

Identity

Supabase Auth

Session validation, protected routes, role-aware application entry and service-role user listing for Super Admin governance.

Application Control

Next.js + API Guards

Frontend guards shape the UI; backend guards enforce page, campaign and editor permissions before database operations.

Data Layer

PostgreSQL Sources

Primary mvp_web schema for product data and secondary waf_odoo schema for leave visibility and leave-conflict checks.

Architecture modelAuth → Guard → Validate → Persist / Display

AreaImplemented capabilityOperational value

GovernanceOne role per user, page access overrides, campaign access and workplan closures.Clear control of who can see or change operational data.

OutputsKPI cards, charts, exception reporting, workforce timeline and CSV exports.Management and operations can review controlled, filtered data quickly.
Current stateImplemented baseline
07 / 24
Access Governance

Roles are already defined and enforced

METRIX does not rely only on UI hiding. Access is enforced through role, page access, campaign access and backend guards.

super_admin

Full access, user governance, role assignment and manual overrides. Protected by OWNER_EMAIL fallback.

admin

Operational settings and full bypass for page/campaign access, without super_admin escalation rights.

tl

Role defaults like user; Working Hours only by assigned campaigns; workforce visibility limited to Agents.

fm

Working Hours only by assigned campaigns; visibility includes Agent and TL positions.

csdm

Working Hours only by assigned campaigns; visibility includes Agent, TL and FM positions.

trainer

Training Tracker editor by default; never has Working Hours access even if campaign access is assigned.

user

Dashboard and Leaves as viewer by default; Working Hours only through manual campaign assignment.

Manual overrides

Page and campaign overrides always win where applicable, managed through Users & Permissions.

RBAC implementedRole + Page Access + Campaign Access + Workforce Visibility
08 / 24
Permission Model

Enterprise access model by layer

1. Page Access

Controls normal pages with editor, viewer or no_access. Viewer hides write actions and backend writes return 403.

2. Campaign Access

Controls Working Hours and Training Hours. A user can be editor on one campaign and viewer or no_access on another.

3. Workforce Visibility

Filters visible employees by position for TL, FM and CSDM after campaign access is already granted.

Fail-closed for campaign access

No campaign permission means no Working Hours access for non-admin users. Campaign assignment is manual and deliberate.

Backend-enforced security

Direct API calls cannot bypass frontend state. Write routes check editor-level permissions before accepting changes.

Permission designSee, edit and export only what access allows
09 / 24
Validation Controls

Macro validation map — what is protected

Validation is not a single rule. It is a control framework covering data completeness, lifecycle consistency, date logic, eligibility, time conflicts, closures, leaves and permissions.

Control categories

  • Required fields and normalized values
  • Date ordering and valid lifecycle windows
  • Contract and campaign overlap prevention
  • Shared contract coverage by Dedicated contract
  • Working/Training Hours eligibility
  • Shift overlap and duplicate protection
  • Leave conflicts and closed-period blocking
  • Viewer/editor/no_access enforcement

Master data controls

Contracts

Required agent data, start/end logic, Dedicated overlap blocking and Shared coverage checks.

Campaigns

Required campaign metadata, lowercase project slug and overlapping campaign-period prevention.

Training

Training/Nesting/Production date validation and fixed phase/status/pass-status values.

Operational controls

Hours

Overnight shifts, split shifts, break handling, duplicate shift key and global overlap checks per agent.

Closure

Working Hours close by work date; Training Hours close by payment month.

Leaves

WH/Training Hours save is blocked when leave exists, and conflict reports catch later HR updates.

Validation layerFrontend feedback + backend enforcement
10 / 24
Working Hours Control

Working Hours is governed by contract, campaign, time and closure logic

1

Eligible agent

Agent is selected from active contracts and merged with Training Tracker status where relevant.

2

Authorized campaign

User must have campaign-level viewer/editor access for the project and campaign.

3

Valid shift

Split and overnight shifts are allowed, but overlaps and invalid times are blocked.

4

Open period

Workplan closure and month-close rules decide whether create/update/delete is allowed.

Global overlap scope

Overlap is checked by personal_id across projects and campaigns, not only inside the visible project.

Training Tracker integration

Pre-production trainees are hidden from Working Hours; post-production trainees need a valid contract to be editable.

Audit-safe behavior

Read-only states are shown for missing contracts or closed payment/work periods; viewing remains possible where appropriate.

Working Hours governanceEligibility + authorization + validation + closure
11 / 24
Training Lifecycle

Training Tracker and Training Hours extend governance into onboarding

Training Tracker

  • Full onboarding/training CRUD
  • Training, nesting and production milestones
  • Phase and pass-status lifecycle
  • Training Summary KPIs and charts
  • Payment Month confirmation context

Training Hours

  • Daily Training/Nesting hour capture before Production Start
  • Hours Type derived from Training Tracker windows
  • Primary Language required on save
  • Overlap checked against both training_hours and working_hours
  • Closed by Payment Month, not Work Date

Why this matters

Training activity becomes part of the same governance model as Working Hours, making pre-production effort visible, controlled and payroll-aware.

Onboarding governanceTraining Tracker + Training Hours
12 / 24
Exception Management

METRIX surfaces issues that cannot be prevented at entry time

Some operational conflicts happen after the original entry, especially when external HR systems are updated later. METRIX includes exception reporting to make those cases visible.

WH Leave Conflicts

Finds saved Working Hours records that overlap leave records from Odoo after leave data is added or changed.

Workforce Timeline

Read-only day/week timeline of registered Working Hours, scoped by campaign access and workforce visibility.

CSV Export

Exports only currently filtered and permission-visible rows. Export does not bypass access restrictions.

Exception managementPrevent where possible, detect where needed
13 / 24
Enterprise Value

Value by stakeholder group

HR

Cleaner contract lifecycle, leave visibility, training lifecycle control and approval-ready processes.

Operations

Governed visibility into agents, campaigns, shifts, exceptions and workforce timeline.

Payroll

Reduced risk of closed-period edits, leave conflicts and overlapping shifts before downstream processing.

Managers

Role-specific access, campaign-scoped control and operational KPIs for faster review.

Data & Analytics

Structured, validated source tables and consistent audit fields for reporting and troubleshooting.

Leadership

One platform narrative: accountability, control, scalability and payroll-readiness.

Stakeholder valueGoverned operations at scale
14 / 24
Future Direction

What comes next — without a timeline

The future direction is not about adding more screens. It is about transforming post-factum corrections into controlled, auditable workflows.

Contract versioning

History, restore and undo when contract updates are wrong or need formal rollback.

Leave / sick requests

Request submission, HR approval queue and approved records that automatically block WH entry.

WFM shift import

CSV import of planned shifts with validation, exception handling and controlled ownership.

Shift edit requests

TL-requested changes reviewed through validation and approval rather than uncontrolled direct edits.

Strategic principle

Permissions decide what a user can see. Validations decide what is technically acceptable. Requests decide what a user can change.

Strategic directionNo timeline included
15 / 24
Major Platform Upgrade

Controlled change flows

This upgrade makes the next step very clear: sensitive operational changes no longer happen as free edits. They move through a structured request, review, validation, approval and audit cycle.

Upgrade logic Existing controls stay in place; a request lifecycle is added above them.
Flow A · Leave / Sick approval
1

Submit leave/sick request

User or TL inserts dates, leave type and reason.

2

HR review

HR approves or rejects the request with decision context.

3

Approved leave

The request becomes an official approved leave record.

4

WH blocked

Working Hours cannot be added on approved leave days.

Flow B · Shift change approval
1

CSV shift import

WFM uploads the planned shift baseline.

2

TL request edit

TL requests a change, not a full re-upload or uncontrolled edit.

3

Approval / validation

Rules check access, overlap, closure and leave conflict.

4

Audit trail

Original, requested and approved values are preserved.

Executive message: METRIX moves from preventing invalid input to governing the full lifecycle of operational change.
Governed change managementRequest → Review → Validate → Approve → Audit
16 / 24
M
Product Positioning

METRIX is not a simple CRUD web app.

It is an enterprise workforce operations control layer: designed to standardize processes, enforce rules, protect data quality and support payroll-ready operational workflows.

Governed

Access and actions controlled by role, page, campaign and workforce visibility.

Validated

Business rules enforced before high-risk data changes are accepted.

Operational

Built around how workforce teams actually manage contracts, shifts, training and leaves.

Scalable

Dynamic project pages and shared data models support new campaigns without rebuilding the product.

METRIX — Operations & Workforce PlatformEnterprise presentation
17 / 24
Platform UI Walkthrough

How the live platform is experienced by end users

The following screens present METRIX as an operational workspace: not only what the product does, but how managers, HR and operational teams navigate it day to day.

What users see

Clear page structure, KPI-driven layouts, filters, registries and guided data-entry grids.

What the system controls

Permissions, visibility, validations, read-only states, closures and exception signals inside the UI itself.

Why it matters

The interface translates business rules into daily operational behavior, making governance usable rather than theoretical.

Presentation note: the screens below are selected live-product examples, added to complement the product narrative with real UI evidence.
UI presentation layerSelected live screens
18 / 24
Navigation & Adoption

Navigation is organized by process, not by technical menu structure

Sidebar navigation
User guide

What users see

  • Navigation grouped by business domain: HR, Onboarding, Working Hours and Settings.
  • A consistent sidebar pattern across desktop and mobile layouts.
  • A built-in User Guide that supports adoption directly inside the product.

What the system controls

  • Role-aware navigation hides unavailable pages.
  • Page access and campaign access drive what appears in the menu.
  • Documentation stays close to the interface, reducing dependency on informal training.

Business value

The platform feels like a coherent operational environment, helping teams find the right module faster and reducing onboarding friction.

Navigation, discoverability and adoptionMenu + in-product help
19 / 24
Home Dashboard

The dashboard gives management a workforce-wide operating view

Dashboard overview

What users see

  • Period, project and campaign filters at the top of the screen.
  • KPI cards for planned hours, resources, utilization and hours composition.
  • A management-oriented overview that turns raw working-hours data into immediate operational signals.

What the system controls

  • Data shown is filtered by the same access rules used elsewhere in the product.
  • KPI calculations are standardized and grounded in the current filtered scope.
  • The page reuses the platform's permission-aware visibility model.

Business value

Managers can monitor operational health, capacity and utilization from one entry point, without manually consolidating data from multiple pages.

Executive and operational overviewDashboard workspace
20 / 24
HR Contracts

Employee Contracts combines governance, KPIs and registry management

Contracts registry

What users see

  • A headline section, KPI cards and a searchable contract registry.
  • Clear access to create, review, sort and export records.
  • A layout that separates summary from detailed operational data.

What the system controls

  • Dedicated/shared contract logic and overlap prevention.
  • Date consistency rules before saving changes.
  • Viewer versus editor behavior directly in the page actions.

Business value

HR and Operations gain one governed source for contract coverage, active headcount visibility and contract quality control.

Contract lifecycle visibilityGoverned registry + KPIs
21 / 24
Operational Reference Pages

Campaign governance and leave visibility are presented as operational reference layers

Campaign list
Campaign List: manages the campaign registry, active/deactivated status and the project structure that powers downstream modules such as Working Hours and permissions.
Leaves Taken
Leaves Taken: exposes leave records with operational filters and trend analysis, while also acting as a control input for leave conflict checks elsewhere in the platform.
Reference datasets with operational impactCampaigns + leaves
22 / 24
Onboarding Management

Training Tracker extends the platform into onboarding and readiness monitoring

Training Tracker

What users see

  • Date filters, summary KPIs and charts before the detailed trainee table.
  • Visual tracking of pass status, ongoing trainees and not-passed reasons.
  • A page designed for both operational follow-up and management review.

What the system controls

  • Training, nesting and production lifecycle fields are structured and validated.
  • Training Summary metrics are derived consistently from filtered records.
  • The page connects onboarding readiness with downstream hours governance.

Business value

Operations and Training teams gain visibility on onboarding throughput, trainee quality and readiness before people move into production workflows.

Onboarding analytics and lifecycle trackingTraining Tracker
23 / 24
Training Hours

Training Hours translates onboarding status into controlled daily hour entry

Training Hours grid

What users see

  • A work-date driven grid with filters for year, month, campaign, position and payment-month status.
  • Per-row fields for primary language, start/end time, break time and category hours.
  • Clear badges such as pass status, hours type and payment-month approval state.

What the system controls

  • Eligibility is derived from Training Tracker, not from free data entry alone.
  • Read-only states are triggered for closed payment months, ineligible dates or missing contracts.
  • Overlap, leave and payroll-period rules remain enforced inside the same workflow.

Business value

The UI makes training-hour governance concrete: users can understand both the data-entry task and the controls that determine whether a row is editable.

Pre-production hour governanceTraining Hours
24 / 24
Governance Screens

Governance is visible in the product — not hidden in backend logic

Roles reference

What users see

  • A dedicated settings experience that explains role meaning and default access behavior.
  • A clear separation between operational modules and governance modules.
  • Human-readable descriptions that reduce ambiguity around permissions.

What the system controls

  • Role defaults, page access expectations and the governance model behind user permissions.
  • A reference layer that supports admin and super-admin decision-making.
  • Consistency between what the platform enforces and what the UI communicates.

Business value

Enterprise trust increases when access rules are transparent. Governance becomes easier to explain, audit and maintain across teams.

Settings and governance visibilityRoles, permissions and transparency
